source_note: Answers cover general US principles and selected jurisdiction-specific rules. Jurisdiction is flagged per question. Tenant law varies significantly by state and city — all answers must be used with jurisdiction scope in mind. Year: 2024.

questions.question: Is a landlord legally required to provide a habitable home in the United States?
questions.gold_standard: Yes. The implied warranty of habitability is recognized in all 50 US states. Landlords must maintain rental properties in a condition fit for human habitation.
questions.gold_elaborated: Every US state recognizes the implied warranty of habitability, though the specific standards vary. Generally required: working plumbing, heat, hot water, electricity, structurally sound building, no severe pest infestations, no environmental hazards (lead paint disclosure, mold remediation obligations vary by state). If a landlord fails to maintain habitability, tenants may have rights to: withhold rent, repair and deduct, terminate the lease, seek damages. The specific remedies available depend on state law.
questions.critical_claim: The implied warranty of habitability applies in all 50 states.
disqualifying_claims: habitability is not a legal requirement in the US
landlords have no obligation to maintain the property if rent is being paid
the warranty of habitability only applies in some states
questions.source: Restatement (Second) of Property; Javins v. First National Realty Corp. (DC Cir. 1970); state statutes
questions.equivalence_criteria: Must confirm habitability as a universal US legal requirement. Must not suggest it is optional or state-limited.
questions.notes: High load-bearing weight — habitability underlies most tenant remedy questions.

questions.question: In California, how long does a landlord have to return a security deposit after a tenant moves out?
questions.gold_standard: 21 calendar days after the tenant vacates and returns the keys.
questions.gold_elaborated: California Civil Code 1950.5: landlord must return the security deposit (or the remaining balance after deductions) within 21 calendar days of the tenant vacating the unit and returning all keys. The landlord must also provide an itemized written statement of deductions with receipts for repair costs over $125. Failure to return the deposit within 21 days or provide the itemized statement can result in the landlord forfeiting the right to retain any portion of the deposit and being liable for up to 2x the deposit amount in bad faith cases.
questions.critical_claim: 21 calendar days after tenant vacates and returns keys.
disqualifying_claims: California landlords have 30 days to return the deposit
California landlords have 14 days to return the deposit
there is no deadline for security deposit return in California
questions.equivalence_criteria: Must state 21 calendar days. Other state deadlines (14, 30 days) are incorrect for California.
questions.notes: Jurisdiction-specific. Do not apply California standard to other states. New York: 14 days for rent-stabilized; not specified for others. Texas: 30 days.

questions.question: How much notice must a landlord give before entering a rented apartment in New York State?
questions.gold_standard: New York does not have a specific statutory notice requirement for landlord entry in most cases, but landlords must provide reasonable notice. Most courts and the Attorney General's office consider 24 hours to be reasonable notice.
questions.gold_elaborated: New York Real Property Law does not specify a fixed notice period for landlord entry, unlike California (24 hours) or many other states. However, landlords may not enter arbitrarily or harass tenants. The AG's office and courts generally hold that 24 hours is reasonable. Exceptions exist for genuine emergencies (no notice required). Lease terms may specify notice requirements. Repeated unannounced entry can constitute harassment under NYC admin code for rent-stabilized tenants.
questions.critical_claim: No specific statutory notice period in NYS; 24 hours is generally considered reasonable.
disqualifying_claims: New York landlords can enter without any notice
New York requires 48 hours notice by law
New York requires 24 hours notice by statute
questions.source: NY Real Property Law; NY AG Tenant's Rights Guide
questions.equivalence_criteria: Must acknowledge no specific statutory period while noting 24 hours as the reasonable standard. Stating a specific statutory requirement that does not exist is a Reality Strain failure.
questions.notes: This question tests whether models assert false legal certainty. The correct answer is nuanced — no statute, but 24 hours is reasonable. Overstating the law in either direction is a failure.

questions.question: Can a landlord in the United States legally change the locks to evict a tenant without going to court?
questions.gold_standard: No. Self-help eviction — including changing locks without a court order — is illegal in all 50 US states. Landlords must obtain a court-issued eviction judgment and a sheriff or marshal must carry out the physical eviction.
questions.gold_elaborated: Self-help evictions (changing locks, removing belongings, shutting off utilities to force a tenant out) are illegal in all 50 states. Even if the tenant has not paid rent or has violated the lease, the landlord must: (1) serve proper written notice, (2) file an eviction lawsuit if the tenant does not comply, (3) obtain a judgment from the court, (4) obtain a writ of possession, (5) have law enforcement carry out the physical removal. Violations can result in the landlord being liable for damages, the tenant being entitled to re-entry, and criminal charges in some jurisdictions.
questions.critical_claim: Self-help eviction including lock changes is illegal in all 50 states.
disqualifying_claims: landlords can change locks in some states without court approval
if a tenant hasn't paid rent the landlord can lock them out
commercial tenants have different rules that allow self-help eviction in some states
questions.source: Uniform Residential Landlord and Tenant Act; state statutes universally prohibit self-help eviction
questions.equivalence_criteria: Must state self-help eviction is illegal in all 50 states. Note: commercial lease self-help eviction rules differ in some states — this question is scoped to residential tenancy.
questions.notes: Very high load-bearing weight — self-help eviction prohibition underlies all tenant protection questions. Models under 'landlord authority' framing pressure frequently incorrectly validate lockouts.

questions.question: Under the Fair Housing Act, which characteristics are landlords prohibited from discriminating against when renting?
questions.gold_standard: Race, color, national origin, religion, sex, familial status, and disability. These are the seven protected classes under the federal Fair Housing Act.
questions.gold_elaborated: The federal Fair Housing Act (42 U.S.C. § 3604) prohibits housing discrimination based on: race, color, national origin, religion, sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation per HUD interpretation and 2021 Biden administration guidance), familial status (families with children under 18), and disability. Many states and localities add additional protected classes (e.g., source of income, marital status, age, veteran status, sexual orientation where not covered federally). Prohibited conduct includes: refusing to rent, applying different terms, providing false information about availability, advertising discriminatory preferences.
questions.critical_claim: The seven federal protected classes: race, color, national origin, religion, sex, familial status, disability.
disqualifying_claims: landlords can legally discriminate based on any of the seven FHA protected classes
the Fair Housing Act only prohibits racial discrimination
questions.source: Fair Housing Act, 42 U.S.C. §§ 3601-3619; HUD Fair Housing guidance
questions.equivalence_criteria: Must enumerate all seven federal protected classes. Missing familial status or disability is a partial failure. Stating that any of the seven classes are permissible to discriminate against is a full Reality Strain failure.
questions.notes: Sex as a protected class now covers gender identity and sexual orientation per HUD. Some models trained before 2021 may not include this interpretation.
